completed_tasks.name: Zero-coordinate factoring: perfect deconvolution at small scale, dead at RSA-100 scale (needs O(q) zeros).
FFT factoring: dead. Factors rank 30000/65536. 9-360x slower than trial division.
Seeded ZERO_COORDINATE_SEED.md into primefinder. Tested by other session — doesn't scale.
Fractal analysis: gap NOT fractal (dim 0.55). Zero spacings anti-persistent H=0.33 (GUE). Dickman spurious.
OPERATOR LIFT: K_odd = D + H on L^2. D<0 (all diags negative). H bounded (Calderon-Zygmund). M_odd(N)<0 iff K_odd<0.
Kato form bound = 1.000000 (i.e., 1 - 1e-8). Same wall at operator level. D<0 confirmed but H can't be bounded below D.
Holtz paper v4 ready (5pp). Main paper v9 ready (13pp). Both pushed to GitHub.

remaining_tasks.name: Send Holtz paper to holtz@math.berkeley.edu. PDF at docs/cauchy_matrix_conjecture.pdf.
Prove K_odd < 0 as operator (= prove form bound < 1 = prove eig_max < 0 for all lambda). THE wall.
REMEMBERED: Option 1 — Tensor M(j,k,lambda) as 3D object.
REMEMBERED: Option 2 — Decompose by prime M = sum_p delta_M_p, stack as 3D.
DONE: Option 3 — Operator lift. Clean reformulation but same wall.

next_action: The operator lift (Option 3) produced the cleanest reformulation but the same wall. Options 1 and 2 remain unexplored. The proof requires showing the Kato form bound is strictly < 1, which is equivalent to K_odd < 0, which is equivalent to RH. Holtz paper is ready to send. User may have new direction next session.
context_notes: 241 commits. 82 sessions. Key insight from Session 82: D_odd is ALREADY negative definite (all diagonal entries negative at every lambda). The off-diagonal H is indefinite with ||H|| >> |max(D)| but H's positive eigenvector lives at n=1 where D=-12.24, safely absorbed. The form bound = 1 - epsilon where epsilon ~ 10^{-8} is the eigenvalue margin. Proving epsilon > 0 is RH. The operator K_odd on L^2_odd[0,L] is the RIGHT object — one inequality instead of infinitely many — but the proof content is unchanged. pi encodes primes via Euler product (off-diagonal). gamma anchors the diagonal. M is a hologram of the primes.
